question: Will CZ post 160-179 posts from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between March 20, 12:00 PM ET and March 27, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket centers around 280-359 tweets for Elon Musk during March 20-27, 2026, with 300-339 ranges leading at 11.5% implied probabilities each, reflecting his consistent high-volume posting habits—averaging 40-50 posts daily including replies and polls amid Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, and political commentary. Recent weeks show sustained activity near 300, fueled by election aftermath buzz and X platform tweaks boosting engagement, though quieter periods dip below 250. Competitive dynamics hinge on event-driven spikes, like potential Neuralink announcements or viral memes, versus lulls from travel; historical variance keeps mid-300s bins tightly matched, as secret algorithms and algorithmic replies add unpredictability to exact counts.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket centers around 280-359 tweets for Elon Musk during March 20-27, 2026, with 300-339 ranges leading at 11.5% implied probabilities each, reflecting his consistent high-volume posting habits—averaging 40-50 posts daily including replies and polls amid Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, and political commentary. Recent weeks show sustained activity near 300, fueled by election aftermath buzz and X platform tweaks boosting engagement, though quieter periods dip below 250. Competitive dynamics hinge on event-driven spikes, like potential Neuralink announcements or viral memes, versus lulls from travel; historical variance keeps mid-300s bins tightly matched, as secret algorithms and algorithmic replies add unpredictability to exact counts.

question: Ollie Watkins: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Aston Villa FC and West Ham United FC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 10:15 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ollie Watkins is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. events.title: Aston Villa FC vs. West Ham United FC - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Premier League game between Aston Villa FC and West Ham United FC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in Aston Villa vs. West Ham United player props centers on Ollie Watkins' red-hot form, with implied probabilities favoring his anytime goal scorer prop at around 55% after netting in six of his last eight starts. Villa's home strength at Villa Park—unbeaten in 12 league matches—amplifies threats from wingers Leon Bailey and Morgan Rogers for shots-on-target overs. West Ham counters via Jarrod Bowen's volume shooting (averaging 3.2 per game), though Lucas Paqueta's looming suspension risk and Michail Antonio's hamstring absence curb midfield creativity. Recent Villa rest advantage post-midweek cup tie edges momentum, but monitor Friday injury reports for Tielemans and Kudus status shifts.

question: Spread: AZ (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "AZ" if AZ win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "SC Heerenveen".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AZ vs. SC Heerenveen - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AZ Alkmaar's strong home form and third-place standing in the Eredivisie drive trader consensus favoring them against mid-table SC Heerenveen, with markets reflecting about 65% implied probability for an AZ win. Alkmaar enters unbeaten in their last five league matches, scoring freely thanks to Troy Parrott's eight goals, while Heerenveen has lost four of six away games, conceding 2.2 goals per match on average. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, but Heerenveen's suspension of key midfielder Simon Olsson could disrupt their midfield control. Upcoming factors include AZ's rest advantage after midweek cup action and Heerenveen's poor head-to-head record (winless in last five visits to AFAS Stadion), tilting sentiment toward the hosts despite Eredivisie volatility.

events.title: AZ vs. SC Heerenveen - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AZ Alkmaar's strong home form and third-place standing in the Eredivisie drive trader consensus favoring them against mid-table SC Heerenveen, with markets reflecting about 65% implied probability for an AZ win. Upcoming factors include AZ's rest advantage after midweek cup action and Heerenveen's poor head-to-head record (winless in last five visits to AFAS Stadion), tilting sentiment toward the hosts despite Eredivisie volatility.

events.title: Deportivo Toluca FC vs. Atlético San Luis - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 12 at 9:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Toluca's dominant home form in Liga MX Apertura, unbeaten in six matches at Estadio Nemesio Díez, anchors trader consensus favoring them over visiting Atlético San Luis, who have just one away win this season. Toluca hold second place after three consecutive victories, scoring freely with 14 goals in their last five games, while San Luis sit 12th amid three straight losses and defensive woes conceding 2.2 goals per match lately. Official injury reports confirm no new absences for Toluca's key attackers like Alexis Vega, but San Luis misses midfielder Vitinho. Head-to-head history tilts Toluca's way (4-1 in last five), with rest advantage post-midweek for the hosts potentially swaying probabilities.

events.title: Pumas de la UNAM vs. Mazatlán FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 12 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pumas UNAM's dominant home record and recent surge—unbeaten in five straight Estadio Olímpico Universitario matches—anchor trader consensus at around 70% implied probability for a Pumas win versus Mazatlán FC in this Liga MX Apertura clash. Rest advantages favor the hosts after a lighter midweek schedule, heightening upset risk only if Mazatlán exploits counterattacks.

events.title: Miami Open: Matteo Arnaldi vs Alexander Shevchenko
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Matteo Arnaldi and Alexander Shevchenko in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 18 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Matteo Arnaldi' if Matteo Arnaldi advances against Alexander Shevchenko.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Shevchenko' if Alexander Shevchenko advances against Matteo Arnaldi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will the highest temperature in London be 8°C or below on March 21?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 21 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Charles de Gaulle Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/paris/LFPG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a Paris high of 16°C (43%) or 17°C (39.5%) on March 21, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts projecting mild southerly airflow and high-pressure ridging over Western Europe, yielding daytime maxima in that range under partly cloudy skies. These odds reflect a positive temperature anomaly of 4-5°C above the 1991-2020 March 21 climatological average of ~12°C at Paris-Charles de Gaulle, supported by recent model convergence despite minor spread in boundary layer mixing and cloud feedbacks. Lower probabilities for 18°C+ stem from capped convective potential and nocturnal cooling risks, while sub-15°C outcomes are dismissed amid absent cold-air advection signals from official Météo-France outlooks; watch tomorrow's 12Z runs for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble weather model forecasts, particularly from ECMWF and GFS, drive trader consensus toward a São Paulo high of 30°C on March 21 at 32.5% implied probability, edging out 29°C (29%) and 28°C (21%), amid stable autumn conditions with no cold fronts or tropical disturbances. INMET projections align closely at 29-31°C under partly cloudy skies and light southerly winds that temper extremes, while the city's urban heat island amplifies station readings beyond rural baselines (historical March average ~28.5°C). Model spread reflects minor uncertainties in cloud cover and humidity—ECMWF cooler, GFS slightly hotter—differentiating outcomes by 1°C, with low odds for deviations due to consistent soil moisture from recent rains suppressing sharper peaks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors 12°C (39%) and 11°C (32%) as Seoul's highest temperature on March 21, driven by ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models converging on mild late-winter conditions with highs in that narrow band. Korea Meteorological Administration updates show a shallow high-pressure ridge promoting daytime heating to 11-12°C, tempered by light northerly winds and partial cloud cover that caps peaks below 13°C. Historical March 21 averages hover at 11.3°C, but a slight positive temperature anomaly from recent mild air masses tilts odds toward 12°C; differentiation hinges on afternoon insolation and boundary layer mixing, with low-confidence outliers like 14°C+ reflecting model spread amid synoptic uncertainty.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment hinges on the razor-thin split between 4°C (34.5%) and 5°C (35.5%) probabilities, driven by Environment and Climate Change Canada's latest forecast and global model ensembles (ECMWF, GFS, GEM) converging on a daytime high near 4.5°C for Toronto on March 21. Recent 12z model runs show a mild southerly flow and high-pressure ridge nudging temperatures upward from prior cooler outlooks, elevating 6°C or higher odds to 20.5%, though persistent cloud cover and potential afternoon showers differentiate outcomes—limiting solar heating to cap at 5°C versus clearer breaks pushing beyond. Historical March 21 averages hover around 5°C with high variability from jet stream waviness, underscoring resolution uncertainty tied to frontal timing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for Seattle's March 21 highest temperature clusters tightly around 50-51°F (36.5%) and 48-49°F (32%), driven by the latest NWS Seattle forecast implying a peak of 50°F under persistent marine layer influence capping daytime heating. NOAA ensemble models average 49.7°F, with slight spread from GFS (51°F) versus ECMWF (49°F), reflecting uncertainty in low-level northerly winds and afternoon cloud breaks. Historical March 21 highs at Sea-Tac average 54°F, but cooler-than-normal sea surface temperatures this season suppress anomalies by 3-4°F. Differentiation hinges on solar insolation penetrating stratus—full overcast favors 48-49°F, partial clearing tips to 50-51°F—pending 12z model updates.
